If you fail a check by 5 or more, you can recall information that is diametrically opposed to the truth.
If you fail a check by 5 or more, you can recall information that is diametrically opposed to the truth.
If you fail a check by 5 or more, you can recall information that is diametrically opposed to the truth.

fZXJZL92grjstTL7.description:
You have had access to thousands of spy reports, many of which turned out to be based on faulty deductions or even intentionally false stories spread by opposed spy groups. At this point, you have been exposed to false knowledge as much as accurate information, and you can’t always remember which is which.

Effects: You take a -2 penalty on all Knowledge checks, and if you fail a Knowledge check by 5 or more, you can recall information that is diametrically opposed to the truth.
fZXJZL92grjstTL7.name: Information Overload

contextNotes.text: Caster level is treated as [[1]] higher on attempts to dispel one of your spells.

fdutS559VkMuO2s2.description:
Your great force of will makes your magic harder to dispel.

Benefits: Your caster level is treated as 1 level higher whenever someone attempts to dispel one of your spells.
fdutS559VkMuO2s2.name: Resilient Caster

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to find concealed or secret objects (including doors and traps).

ffazVCKCsklGpxHW.description:
You've always had a keen eye for the glitter of gold or silver, and you've robbed enough rich merchants and raided enough ships to have a feel for where the most valuable plunder is hidden. You found yourself relatively well off when you got your share of your last voyage's plunder, and decided to spend a few of your hard-won coins at the Formidably Maid, a popular pirate tavern in Port Peril. Maybe the shine of your new wealth distracted you, however, because you never saw the miscreants who slipped something into your drink, knocking you out cold.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise and Perception checks to find concealed or secret objects (including doors and traps). In addition, you begin the campaign with a selection mundane trade goods worth 50 gp that you were able to scrounge together during your first day on the Wormwood.
ffazVCKCsklGpxHW.name: Eye for Plunder (Skull and Shackles)

fg4mXME2v6OsxSqZ.description:
Through pacts with eldritch beings or a simple quirk of circumstance, your voice has an uncanny rapport with those swept up in madness.

Benefits: Once per day as an immediate action, you can issue a command to a confused creature within 30 feet. This allows the creature to immediately reroll on the confused condition table, and you can pick which of the two actions—the action of its initial roll or that of the reroll—the creature takes. Confused creatures do not need to understand you for this trait to take effect.
fg4mXME2v6OsxSqZ.name: Voices in your Head

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to gather information.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to recall information about criminal activities.

fhCUhy3sAwL6yqe2.description: Benefits: Long exposure to the endless variety of the Nightstalls grants you a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information and on Knowledge (local) checks to recall information about criminal activities. One of these (your choice) is always a class skill for you.
fhCUhy3sAwL6yqe2.name: Nightstalls Navigator

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.

fnG86YNh3IiBhfHN.description:
You followed - or some might say exploited - the rules and earned your freedom by finding the loopholes within your homeland's labyrinthine codes regulating slavery and indentured servitude.

Requirements: Halfling; Molthune, Osirion

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on all Knowledge checks to find a legitimate way to avoid, negate, or escape an existing obligation.
fnG86YNh3IiBhfHN.name: Legalistic Liberation (Halfling; Molthune, Osirion)

contextNotes.text: Whenever you roll a natural 20, with a magic weapon, you gain a +5 trait bonus on the check

fnZWwaMTL2jkX67e.description:
You trained with the Blackjackets of Druma to make optimal use of magic weapons.

Benefits: Whenever you roll a natural 20 (the die shows a “20”) on a combat maneuver check with a magic weapon, you gain a +5 trait bonus on the check.
fnZWwaMTL2jkX67e.name: Blackjacket Weapon Flair

foQQ5u7PzJ2ZtS4b.description:
The skills of Golarion's greatest musicians are at your fingertips, thanks to the vast treasure trove of musical knowledge in the vaults beneath the Grand Lodge in Absalom.

Requirements: Bard, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: Studying this knowledge gave you the ability to use your bardic performance an additional 3 rounds per day.
foQQ5u7PzJ2ZtS4b.name: Maestro of the Society (Bard, Pathfinder Society)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when trying to decipher the ancient Azlanti language.

ftX8GMIMMfVZC3KF.description:
You grew up in wonder of Chelish theater, and spent countless hours in the opera houses across Cheliax and even more time perfecting your voice.

Requirements: Chelaxian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perform (sing) checks. Furthermore you have memorized the librettos of many works sung in the ancient Azlanti tongue, granting you a +2 trait bonus on Linguistics checks when trying to decipher the ancient Azlanti language.
ftX8GMIMMfVZC3KF.name: Operatic (Human; Chelaxian)

fu4vtYS7KLgGuN7q.description:
The hidden coves and bays of the island of Belem have been your home throughout most of your career as a pirate, and the winding waterways of Minata have trained you well in the art of sailing.

Requirements: Minata

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) checks and Profession (sailor) checks, and one of these skills becomes a class skill for you.
fu4vtYS7KLgGuN7q.name: Belem Pirate (Minata)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs. language-dependent and effects with the sonic descriptor.

fx3U8QEHAJ33fSEC.description:
You respect the freedom of others to speak as they will. Your rough-and-tumble youth taught you that everyone deserves a chance to say her piece. After all, you can always simply choose to ignore the speaker.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against language dependent and effects with the sonic descriptor.

In addition, the DC to Intimidate you increases by 2.

Suggested Characters

Andorens, Desnans, Erastil worshipers.
fx3U8QEHAJ33fSEC.name: Free Spirit

fz0awPuq3xKgdEdJ.description:
That which has marked you for power has also given you proof to show others.

Benefits: You may cast light three times per day as a spell-like ability, but this ability only targets the palm of your hand, and it manifests as a glowing sigil representing your ominous birth—a holy or unholy symbol, a demonic symbol, or perhaps some other distinctive marking established by you and your GM.

Those who recognize this symbol have a starting attitude toward you of one step closer to friendly (if they are followers of or scholars studying the sign) or one step closer to hostile (if they are opposed to the sign or its followers).
fz0awPuq3xKgdEdJ.name: Marked by Unknown Forces

contextNotes.text: +2 trait bonus whenever you use cure spells to heal damage
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten your savior: (Koya or Shalelu)

g6Zq7urU0NZL0BLW.description:
At some point in the past, you had a terrifyingly close brush with death. Maybe a goblin nearly killed you during an attack on the town, or perhaps a building struck by a giant's boulder collapsed around you. Whatever the peril was, you would have certainly died if not for the swift actions of Koya or Shalelu, who intervened just in time to save your life. You've never forgotten this, and remain fiercely loyal to the NPC who saved you. Perhaps even more importantly, the method in which she saved you seems to have had an impact on your own skills.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten your savior as well as one of the following:

If Koya saved you, she did so by casting a healing spell on you just before you died, and as a result you gain a +2 trait bonus whenever you use cure spells to heal damage. If it was Shalelu who saved you, she did so by pulling you to safety while simultaneously defeating the peril that almost did you in, and as a result, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks and Acrobatics is a class skill for you.

NPC Choices Koya, Shalelu
g6Zq7urU0NZL0BLW.name: Rescued (Jade Regent)

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may reroll against a charm or compulsion effect. You must take the second result even if it is worse.

gAI8ok9cqkOvmKCr.description:
You have made a solemn oath, and you pursue that oath with headstrong determination.

Benefits: Once per day, you may reroll a saving throw against a charm or compulsion effect. You must take the second result even if it is worse.
gAI8ok9cqkOvmKCr.name: Oathbound

contextNotes.text: Whenever you fail a check, you take a –[[4]] Penalty on that type of check for the next hour.
Whenever you fail a check, you take a –[[4]] Penalty on that type of check for the next hour.

gAePZuufJvK7mRsc.description:
You lack confidence in your abilities or confidence in the universe at large.

Effects: Whenever you fail a skill or ability check, you take a –4 penalty on that type of skill or ability check for the next hour.
gAePZuufJvK7mRsc.name: Doubt

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to avoid falling.

gBNOupNr4zInLusq.description:
You spent your early years exploring and foraging along the coastal cliffs.

Requirements: Cliffs of Fury

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks, and on Reflex saves to avoid falling.
gBNOupNr4zInLusq.name: Cliff Jumper (Cliffs of Fury)

contextNotes.text: When you successfully aid another, your ally adds a +3 bonus (rather than the normal +2 bonus).
When you successfully aid another, your ally adds a +3 bonus (rather than the normal +2 bonus).

gC4uPlwypbJKt9j5.description:
While enslaved in the City of Brass, you watched too many of your friends fall victim to efreet tempers. Now, you help others avoid thorny situations whenever you are able.

Requirements: City of Brass

Benefits: Any time you successfully use the aid another action to help an ally succeed at a Bluff or Escape Artist check, you add a +3 bonus to this check (rather than a +2 bonus).
gC4uPlwypbJKt9j5.name: Solidarity (City of Brass)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against <chose disease or poison>

You are immediately alerted to the presence of aberrations, oozes, and all creatures with the blighted fey and fungal creature templates within 30 feet (though it doesn’t indicate their exact location). You can always act during the surprise round against such creatures, and you aren’t considered flat-footed against such creatures in the first round of combat.

gCkGVZhSZpnwfhtd.description:
You were raised deep in the Fangwood Forest, but in your childhood the Darkblight overtook your community, and blighted fey attacked your friends and family. Even after escaping, you barely survived infection by the otherworldly fungal disease and still bear a terrible scar from your ordeal.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against disease or poison (your choice), and your scar now throbs painfully in the presence of unnatural creatures, immediately alerting you to their presence. This sixth sense alerts you to the presence of aberrations, oozes, and all creatures with the blighted fey and fungal creature templates within 30 feet (though it doesn’t indicate their exact location). You can always act during the surprise round against such creatures, and you aren’t considered flat-footed against such creatures in the first round of combat.
gCkGVZhSZpnwfhtd.name: Blight-Burned (Ironfang Invasion)

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] bonus to checks to make requests.
Once per day, +[[1]] to the save DC of a single spell you cast with the language-dependent descriptor. ([[@resources.influentialSovereignCourt.value]] remaining uses)

gD0Dr8vukuLyYWPc.description:
When you make a suggestion, strangers often assume that it was their idea to begin with.

Requirements: Sovereign Court

Benefits: You gain a +3 bonus on Diplomacy checks to make requests of a creature. Once per day, you may select one single spell you are casting with the language dependent descriptor; you increase the saving throw DC of that spell by 1.
gD0Dr8vukuLyYWPc.name: Influential (Sovereign Court)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when directly interacting with a genie

gDvKjsbNmqsl7hxb.description:
Your lineage features a large number of genie callers, granting you insight into the intricacies of genie binding.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on any Charisma-based checks when directly interacting with a genie.
gDvKjsbNmqsl7hxb.name: Binder's Blood

contextNotes.text: +[[@abilities.wis.mod]] precision damage when you score a critical hit against an undead opponent.

gECdgDuOyug0Iqz9.description:
Requirements: Iomedae, Knight of Ozem

Benefits: You deal additional precision damage equal to your Wisdom modifier when you score a critical hit with a melee weapon against an undead opponent.
gECdgDuOyug0Iqz9.name: Searing Beacon (Iomedae; Ozem)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against poison.

gGRPAbRpLOLPDMJt.description:
You survived a close relative’s betrayal only through quick wits and a strong stomach.

Requirements: Drow

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks and on saving throws against poison.
gGRPAbRpLOLPDMJt.name: Scheming Survivor (Drow)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when interacting with hags.

gHcJqXZykeCxUT4a.description:
Your strange bearing unsettles others.

Requirements: Changeling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks and Intimidate is a class skill for you. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when interacting with hags.
gHcJqXZykeCxUT4a.name: Creepy (Changeling)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to overrun opponents.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against trip attempts.

gHsjPdHTv0oVTT0z.description:
Your ancestors' great size has gifted you with an exceptionally sturdy frame.

Requirements: Hungerseed Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks made to overrun opponents as well as a +1 trait bonus to your CMD against trip attempts.
gHsjPdHTv0oVTT0z.name: Big Boned (Tiefling; Hungerseed)

gIbd4Ofq0axz4YHN.description:
Survival in Geb requires convincing your betters that you would be of more service to them if they let you live another day than if they turn you into an unliving thrall, and you have mastered the art of buying time against undead attacks.

Requirements: Geb

Benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can use Sanctuary as a spell-like ability (caster level = your highest caster level, or 1st if you have no caster level), with a DC equal to 10 + 1/2 your caster level (minimum 0) + your Charisma modifier. Only undead must save to attack you (other creature types are not affected). The effect ends as soon as you attack a creature, as normal.
gIbd4Ofq0axz4YHN.name: Obsequious Morsel (Geb)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

gLJ68RCWzpaHeKWI.description:
At some point in the past, Professor Lorrimor did you a favor under the condition that he would someday call on you to repay it. After he came to your aid, however, you never saw nor heard from him again, leaving you with a sense of unending anticipation that each day might be the day you were asked to return the favor. Yet that day never came, and your fears and anxiety about what the professor would call on you to do abated. Assuming the old man had either forgotten about you or died, you eventually assumed you'd never have to follow through on your end of the bargain. When you received word of the professor's death, and that he had named you specifically in his will, your dread of what he could possibly want from you has grown throughout your entire journey to Ravengro. Years of living with the fear and uncertainty of the unclaimed debt to Professor Lorrimor have inured you to extreme anxiety.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saves against fear effects.
gLJ68RCWzpaHeKWI.name: Making Good on Promises (Carrion Crown)

gQ9RoYriwgWKTIjz.description:
Your birth was particularly painful and difficult for your mother, who needed potent divine magic to ensure that you survived (your mother may or may not have). In any event, that magic infused you from an early age, and you now channel divine energy with greater ease than most.

Benefits: Whenever you channel energy, you gain a +1 trait bonus to the save DC of your channeled energy.
gQ9RoYriwgWKTIjz.name: Sacred Conduit

contextNotes.text: It takes you only 30 minutes to remove the broken condition from a firearm.

gQXFLu1MgMoYYYqh.description:
Your skill with firearms speeds your repair of such weapons.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft (alchemy) and Craft (weapons) checks, and it takes you only 30 minutes to remove the broken condition from a firearm.
gQXFLu1MgMoYYYqh.name: Unblemished Barrel

gRGsmgABopZjrAWh.description:
Requirements: Android

Benefits: Whenever you are affected by a polymorph spell or effect, your nanites adjust completely at a molecular level. For the duration of the polymorph effect, you are treated as only a humanoid for the purpose of effects that target creatures by type. For example, a ranger’s favored enemy bonus against constructs wouldn’t apply to you while you are polymorphed, but nor would spells such as make whole. This trait modifies part of the android’s constructed racial trait, but otherwise that trait is unchanged (a polymorphed android still can’t gain morale bonuses, is still immune to fear, and so on).
gRGsmgABopZjrAWh.name: Cellular Match (Android)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells and abilities created by elves.

gTAiqJWRBBUF1XBk.description:
Your elven relatives were cold to you and you took to rebelling simply to get attention.

Requirements: Half-Elf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against spells and abilities created by elves.
gTAiqJWRBBUF1XBk.name: Experimental Rebel (Half-Elf)

gTR3igg1FAccvQYh.description:
You are used to crewing on many of the different types of ships that sail the Shackles. As a result, you have a knack for quickly determining the weaknesses and strengths of any ship, even ones you’ve never set foot on before.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Profession (sailor) checks, and Profession (sailor) becomes a class skill for you.
gTR3igg1FAccvQYh.name: Ship Aptitude (The Shackles)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against orcs of Belkzen.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against orcs of Belkzen.

gVOSiK4Wxg2bFOPM.description:
You are among the fabled lichblood orcs, and so you command respect among the orcs of Belkzen.

Requirements: Hold of Belkzen, undead bloodrager bloodline

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks made against orcs of Belkzen. You must have the undead bloodrager bloodline to select this trait.
gVOSiK4Wxg2bFOPM.name: Lichblood (Hold of Belkzen)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls against agents of House Thrune and worshipers of Asmodeus
+[[1]] Trait bonus to the save DCs of your spells against agents of House Thrune and worshipers of Asmodeus.

gVfHIfhLtuUwdhxx.description:
You, or perhaps your family, were once worshipers of Asmodeus, but something happened that made you lose your faith. Perhaps your family was asked to give up something dear as a sacrifice, such as the life of a newly born brother or sister. Maybe the church used your family as a scapegoat to cover up a crime someone higher in the church committed. Or perhaps you simply met someone who opened your eyes and showed you the truth—that Asmodeus does not care for his followers, and that the deity of your new religion does. In any event, you left the church, and as a result, your family was punished; they were either put in prison, exiled from Kintargo, or perhaps even executed. Ever since, you’ve vowed to some day get revenge against the church.

Benefits: Choose one:

You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls or you gain a +1 trait bonus on the save DCs of your spells against agents of House Thrune and worshipers of Asmodeus, including most (but not all) devils.
gVfHIfhLtuUwdhxx.name: Ex-Asmodean (Hell's Rebels)

contextNotes.text: You need only exceed the DC by [[2]] to determine whether the item in question has magic properties (although success still does not grant knowledge of the magic item’s abilities).

gWDmJpsf31Fpn2Xc.description:
Your frequent handling of magic items, artifacts, and other enchanted goods has made you an expert at determining when something has magical properties.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks. In addition, you need only exceed the DC of an Appraise check by 2 in order to determine whether the item in question has magic properties (although success still does not grant knowledge of the magic item’s abilities).

Suggested Characters

Drumans, dwarves, Torag worshipers.
gWDmJpsf31Fpn2Xc.name: Keen Appraiser

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to damage rolls whenever you charge.

gX4fKz26Mp5vhzj2.description:
The constant threat of slavers in your youth taught you to attack first and ask questions later.

Requirements: Ifrit

Benefits: Whenever you charge, you gain a +1 trait bonus on your damage roll.
gX4fKz26Mp5vhzj2.name: Hot Headed (Ifrit)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against curses.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on caster level checks to treat an affliction with magic.

gXf4FIuIT02FyQi5.description:
Requirements: Osirion

Benefits: Whenever a caster level check is required to treat an affliction with magic (such as when casting remove curse on a cursed creature or object), you gain a +2 trait bonus on your caster level check. Additionally, you gain a +1 trait bonus on your saving throws against curses.
gXf4FIuIT02FyQi5.name: Curse Queller (Osirion)

gYOgJlRg3ssPqvVG.description:
Your religious training was entwined with your work serving the city watch of a large city, the primary duty of which was standing sentinel on a city wall.

Requirements: Abadar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks and Perception is always a class skill for you.
gYOgJlRg3ssPqvVG.name: Eyes and Ears of the City (Abadar)

contextNotes.text: Any masterwork item you craft has its hardness and break DC increased by 1. This increase is an enhancement bonus.

gdwIKba1hHEU9AEy.description:
You were trained in the style of Azlanti architecture and craft.

Requirements: Azlanti Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (engineering) checks.

In addition, any masterwork item you craft has its hardness and break DC increased by 1. This increase is an enhancement bonus.
gdwIKba1hHEU9AEy.name: Azlanti Engineering (Human; Azlanti)

ggGeZU7iH0vmOunL.description:
The howling gales of the Eye of Abendego were your lullaby as a child, and you quickly learned to anticipate the winds’ danger as well as parse their song.

Requirements: Eye of Abendego, Sylph

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Profession (sailor) checks, and you treat the wind force of environmental Wind effects as one step less powerful for the purposes of determining any penalties on ranged attacks and Fly checks as well as whether you would be checked or blown away.
ggGeZU7iH0vmOunL.name: Abendego Pilot (Eye of Abendego; Sylph)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Racial bonus to continue running and avoid nonlethal damage from a forced march.

ghcJ9WJLbEhktAjw.description:
You have been trained to hunt and fight on the open plains since birth.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity attack rolls made with a shortspear, as well as a +1 racial bonus on Constitution checks to continue running and avoid nonlethal damage from a forced march.
ghcJ9WJLbEhktAjw.name: Savanna Hunter

contextNotes.text: You and your apparent allies take a -[[4]] penalty on Diplomacy checks to interact with law-abiding citizens (except enemies of the authority that accused you).

gmcJr0f67TNMxCGY.description:
You were publicly linked, truthfully or not, to a crime of significant infamy. You draw attention wherever you go and risk imprisonment or worse in the jurisdiction where the crime was committed.

Effects: You and your apparent allies take a -4 penalty on Diplomacy checks to interact with law-abiding citizens (except enemies of the authority that accused you).
gmcJr0f67TNMxCGY.name: Infamous

contextNotes.text: Treat your caster level as 2 higher to determine the range of sonic spells and effects.

gncoggGfxMDPpoIn.description:
You grew up amid powerful storms and mimicked the rolling thunder with your voice.

Requirements: Sylph

Benefits: Treat your caster level as 2 higher to determine the range of sonic spells and effects. Others do not suffer a penalty on Perception checks to hear your voice due to wind.
gncoggGfxMDPpoIn.name: Carrying Voice (Sylph)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when you are wielding a weapon treated with poison.

gtl3DFfXjQcT9DdL.description:
You know the secret of plunging a poisoned weapon in just the right spot.

Requirements: Norgorber

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls when you are wielding a weapon treated with poison.
gtl3DFfXjQcT9DdL.name: Poisonous Slayer (Norgorber)

gu5atbcLaiYnSQHU.description:
You are familiar with an aspect of Azlanti lore. Maybe you first became enamored with this ancient culture after stumbling across a copy of the first volume of the Pathfinder Chronicles or after learning about Azlant in a university or another type of formal education. Perhaps your parents or guardians were scholars and you’ve grown up hearing about and reading about the ancient Azlanti. For years you dreamed about visiting the continent’s ruins and returning with a discovery that could propel your career. Due to this scholarly bent, the Bountiful Venture Company selected you to join the colony at Talmandor's Bounty .

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history), Knowledge (local), and Use Magic Device checks, and one of these skills is a class skill for you. In addition, you gain Azlanti as a bonus language.
gu5atbcLaiYnSQHU.name: Azlanti Scholar (Ruins of Azlant)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to get a hunch.

gxqfCYfs0CZ1xRl8.description:
You learned to take care of yourself on the streets and know the warning signs of hostile intent.

Requirements: Any City

Benefits: Sense Motive is always a class skill for you, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to get a hunch.
gxqfCYfs0CZ1xRl8.name: Street Wary (Any City)

gyZegJ9ctVXfzfXq.description:
Constant drills and preparation allow you to get more out of your innate magic.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: Whenever you use a spell-like ability gained through your tiefling heritage, it automatically acts as if affected by the Extend Spell metamagic feat.
gyZegJ9ctVXfzfXq.name: Prolong Magic (Tiefling)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made against orcs.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made against orcs.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made against orcs.

h0hgixgyXu3Oj2mN.description:
You have an innate ability to weave stories about yourself that extol your might and prowess.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Bluff, Intimidate, and Perform (oratory) checks made against orcs.
h0hgixgyXu3Oj2mN.name: Big Mouth (Half-Orc, Orc)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls when you are flanking a foe with an ally.

h13NR5ehJSQcbRRX.description:
You were born and raised in the town of Longacre on the southwestern fringes of the Whisperwood, or at least have lived there long enough that you’re considered a native. You’ve never been one for the quiet life, and have been considered a miscreant and reprobate since you were a child. As an adult, you’ve made something of a name for yourself as a thug, enforcer, and petty thief in town, hiring yourself out to anybody who needs muscle in exchange for a few coins. In the course of such employment, you’ve worked with another local troublemaker named Cimri Staelish and have become friends. Cimri has recently gotten involved with some shadowy, though powerful, characters, and as Hell’s Vengeance begins, you and a group of other ne’erdo- wells have agreed to help Cimri burgle a tannery just outside town. With luck, this could be the start of bigger and better things than just working as hired muscle.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate is a class skill for you. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls when you are flanking a foe with an ally.
h13NR5ehJSQcbRRX.name: Local Tough (Hell's Vengeance)

contextNotes.text: When you fail a saving throw against a fey’s spell-like or supernatural ability, you may reroll the save. ([[@resources.accustomedToTrickeryDarkmoonWood.value]] remaining uses)

h3W4fjkrvdYDhtkv.description:
You have been exposed to fey magic and know tricks to avoid it.

Requirements: Darkmoon Wood

Benefits: Once per day, when you fail a saving throw against a fey’s spell-like or supernatural ability, you may reroll the save. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.
h3W4fjkrvdYDhtkv.name: Accustomed to Trickery (Darkmoon Wood)

h3Y7yYus5bF3z90S.description:
You studied with the alchemists of Oenopion, perfecting your craft in the homunculus factories, golemworks, and ooze colony.

Requirements: Nex

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft (alchemy) checks.
h3Y7yYus5bF3z90S.name: Oenopion Alchemist (Nex)

hAbIKZX6nx6fKcxm.description: Effects: You cast no shadow whatsoever, or the shadow you do have is monstrous. Under normal lighted conditions, this is not hard to observe—but uncommon to notice. Creatures that succeed at a DC 15 Wisdom check notice it plainly (an additional Perception check may be required based on environmental conditions). This telltale sign of wickedness cannot be concealed by misdirection, nondetection, or illusions, except those that also affect shadows (such as invisibility).
hAbIKZX6nx6fKcxm.name: Umbral Unmasking

hDjklTDBp5WApRvs.description:
You can change the fortunes of others, typically for the best.

Requirements: Nivi Rhombodazzle

Benefits: Once a day, when an adjacent ally makes a skill check and doesn't like the result, you can allow him a reroll as an immediate action. Your ally must take the result of the reroll, even if it is worse than the original roll.
hDjklTDBp5WApRvs.name: Agent of Chance (Nivi Rhombodazzle)

contextNotes.text: Once per day as a swift action, you can brush your fingers against a closed door or stone wall up to 1 foot thick. Doing so allows you to ignore penalties on Perception checks to listen through the designated object for 1 minute.

hEGGJ7aXifksH1i4.description:
The rajahs of Jalmeray favored you for your stately physique and flawless skin, and so endorsed your candidacy for tutelage at the Conservatory on the island of Grand Sarret.

Requirements: Jalmeray

Benefits: Trained to be the perfect bard, courtesan, or undercover operative, you gain a +1 trait bonus on two Charisma-based skills of your choice. In addition, once per day as a swift action, you can brush your fingers against a closed door or stone wall up to 1 foot thick. Doing so allows you to ignore penalties on Perception checks to listen through the designated object for 1 minute.
hEGGJ7aXifksH1i4.name: Alabaster Odalisque (Jalmeray)

hFAEnJmPJ21hw4oh.description:
You’ve been part of one of Mengkare’s minor experiments.

Benefits: Once per day, you can cast detect psychic significance or telekinetic projectile as a spell-like ability, with a caster level equal to your character level.
hFAEnJmPJ21hw4oh.name: Untapped Potential

hFPZTW74AF3mC16p.description:
You are always ready for bloodshed.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative and Intimidate checks.
hFPZTW74AF3mC16p.name: Bloody-Minded

contextNotes.text: You can always take 10 on checks, even in combat.

hHBPXQSPYZbeu581.description:
You can use your unearthly nature to frighten others.

Requirements: Ifrit

Benefits: You can always take 10 on Intimidate checks, even in combat.
hHBPXQSPYZbeu581.name: Fiery Glare (Ifrit)

hHqyigaIRpRaULYj.description:
You have gained favor with the government of Sothis.

Requirements: Associated with the court of the Black Dome in Osiria

Benefits: Once per week, you can attempt a DC 20 Diplomacy or Intimidate check to gain access to an exclusive site or event, such as a government building or an aristocrat’s ball.
hHqyigaIRpRaULYj.name: Bureaucrat's Favored (Black Dome)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on grapple combat maneuver checks to pin an opponent.

hIClOz4yGOF3QPbv.description:
Like your undead ancestor, you possess an intense yearning for the touch of the living, and the hunger that dwells within you grants an unnatural strength to your grip.

Requirements: Dhampir

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on grapple combat maneuver checks to pin an opponent.
hIClOz4yGOF3QPbv.name: Final Embrace (Dhampir)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when you are the subject of any sonic effect that grants a morale bonus on attack or damage rolls.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to CMB to bull rush, grapple, or reposition maneuvers when you are the subject of any sonic effect that grants a morale bonus on attack or damage rolls.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to CMD to bull rush, grapple, or reposition maneuvers when you are the subject of any sonic effect that grants a morale bonus on attack or damage rolls.

hLW09Pc7lJO8Utk7.description:
Songs of your glory and prowess bolster your strength.

Requirements: Kurgess

Benefits: When you are the subject of any sonic effect that grants a morale bonus on attack or damage rolls, you also gain a +1 trait bonus on Strength-based skill checks; on combat maneuver checks to bull rush, grapple, and reposition; and to your CMD against those combat maneuvers.
hLW09Pc7lJO8Utk7.name: Strength's Fanfare (Kurgess)

contextNotes.text:
+[[1]] Trait bonus 
on attack and damage rolls against undead creatures.

hN8DQkAUFpI6MDYo.description:
Requirements: Dhampir

Benefits: Filled with wrath against the monsters whose unclean blood makes you an outcast, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack and damage rolls against undead creatures.
hN8DQkAUFpI6MDYo.name: Undead Slayer (Dhampir)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against creatures affected by one of your charm or compulsion effects.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against creatures affected by one of your charm or compulsion effects.

hNBUtCJ8VCxZQ1vt.description:
Requirements: Calistria

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks against creatures affected by one of your charm or compulsion effects.
hNBUtCJ8VCxZQ1vt.name: Enchanting Conniver (Calistria)

hPkSd5bvz7rouHD2.description:
You belong to a noble family that matters in Taldor, though as the story begins you may or may not be on good terms with your relatives. Your upbringing among the city’s well-to-do gives you an upper hand when it comes to knowledge of high society, and you start the game with a modest inheritance. With Princess Eutropia’s efforts to provide for Taldor’s common citizens and overturn years of tradition, new lines are being quietly drawn in the sand, and you have found yourself embroiled in these intrigues whether you intended to be or not.

Requirements: With this trait, the assumption is that you belong to a minor noble family (and can make up your family name). In this case, your family keeps a small manor in Aroden’s View or Senate’s Hill. If you want to be a member of one of Oppara’s major noble families, you must take the Noble Scion feat at 1st level.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise and Knowledge (nobility) checks, and one of these skills is always a class skill for you. The Noble Scion feat (see the sidebar) does not have a Charisma prerequisite for you. In addition, you start play with a noble’s outfit, a signet ring, and a single additional nonmagical item worth no more than 200 gp.
hPkSd5bvz7rouHD2.name: Child of Oppara (War for the Crown)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on opposed Charisma checks against devils conjured with planar binding spells.

hTClNMyj07wD1h7W.description:
Hell's Vengeance Player's Guide pg. 12

It’s no secret that conjuring and binding devils is a popular pursuit in Cheliax, and you have served as an apprentice to a diabolist since a young age. Eventually, however, you decided you wanted more for yourself than a life of study and service, and when you felt you had learned all that you were likely to from your master, you struck out on your own. You know you can achieve the loftiest heights of power with the support of Hell behind you, just as you are aware you can fall to the lowest depths if you’re incautious and lose that support. You are determined to use your skills and abilities to bind Hell and its minions to your will, and to make a name for yourself as one of Cheliax’s premier devilbinders.

Benefits: When you cast a summon monster spell to summon a devil or fiendish creature, the duration of that spell increases by 1 round. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on opposed Charisma checks against devils conjured with planar binding spells, and payments to devils you conjure with planar ally spells are reduced by 10%.
hTClNMyj07wD1h7W.name: Apprentice Devilbinder (Hell's Vengeance)

hUYZdqtdASDuqOMZ.description:
Spending time in the “unstuck” part of Uringen has made you more adept at using warps in time and space.

Requirements: Uringen

Benefits: Increase your caster level by 2 when casting spells with the teleportation descriptor.
hUYZdqtdASDuqOMZ.name: Unstuck (Uringen)

hW5fCziKoigBBaEw.description:
Negotiating with other creatures, whether they be animals or people from far-flung realms, is second nature to you.

Requirements: The Concordance

Benefits: Either Diplomacy or Handle Animal becomes a class skill for you. Add your choice of Aklo, Aquan, Auran, Ignan, Sylvan, or Terran as a bonus language to your list of languages known.
hW5fCziKoigBBaEw.name: Natural Negotiator (The Concordance)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against charm or compulsion effects.

hWbFoP0taL4WUGNp.description:
Your resolute devotion to the unadulterated purity of your goddess protects you from those who would manipulate you.

Requirements: Shelyn

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against charm or compulsion effects.
hWbFoP0taL4WUGNp.name: Unswaying Love (Shelyn)

hY8sd1bI9vTdkSld.description:
You were the child of a gunsmith or alchemist and always enjoyed experimenting with fiery alchemical items and minor explosives.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made with thrown splash weapons and alchemist bombs.

Suggested Characters

Alkenstar natives, Katapeshi, Thuvians.
hY8sd1bI9vTdkSld.name: Firebug

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to force a creature to act friendly towards you and can attempt such checks in half the normal time.

hh2FMtMTkXFMKONJ.description:
The Sable Company has taught you to quickly get compliance from those in your way.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks to force a creature to act friendly towards you, and can attempt such checks in half the normal time.
hh2FMtMTkXFMKONJ.name: Cutting Condescension

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to avoid nonlethal damage from cold environments.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against spells with the cold descriptor.

hh71zj3tsruCpIyB.description:
Your family has a long tradition of producing excellent sentries and twilight speakers.

Requirements: Snowcaster Elf Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws made to avoid nonlethal damage from cold environments, and gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against spells with the cold descriptor.
hh71zj3tsruCpIyB.name: Snowblooded (Elf; Snowcaster)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Shield bonus to AC whenever you wield an improvised weapon. If you use the improvised weapon to attack, you lose this shield bonus until the beginning of your next turn.

hjj96WwbW2UIrncH.description:
You’re never defenseless, even when you are caught off guard.

Benefits: Whenever you wield an improvised weapon, you gain a +1 shield bonus to AC. If you use the improvised weapon to attack, you lose this shield bonus until the beginning of your next turn.
hjj96WwbW2UIrncH.name: Improvised Defense

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks made with improvised weapons.

hl4mVwmHWZmr6giU.description:
You come from a family of farmers, but are determined to fight for your rights.

Requirements: Shokuro

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks made with improvised weapons. If you do not possess the Catch Off-Guard or Throw Anything feats, you still take penalties on these attack rolls as normal.
hl4mVwmHWZmr6giU.name: Freedom Fighter (Shokuro)

hm37Z49q7baMWX8Q.description:
Your body has clumps of ugly fur, a sure sign of wolf blood! You win!

Requirements: Goblin, Zarongel

Benefits: Once per day as a standard action, you can take a deep breath through your nose to gain the scent ability for 1 minute.
hm37Z49q7baMWX8Q.name: Wolf Cub (Zarongel)

hsLqFO1eJbnKf3hv.description:
Adventurous scholars raised you, or perhaps you were born during an expedition and haven’t known any other life. You get fidgety and impatient when you become too familiar with a certain place. You are used to having excitement and the quest for knowledge guides your path in life, and you picked up a number of skills along the way that relate to this lifestyle. During your interview with the Bountiful Venture Company, they were impressed with the diverse locations you’ve visited in your life and selected you because of your skills in maneuvering uncharted territory.

Benefits: Choose one Knowledge skill when you gain this trait. You gain a +1 trait bonus to check with that Knowledge skill and Survival checks, and either that Knowledge skill or Survival is a class skill for you. In addition, you treat the machete as a simple weapon.
hsLqFO1eJbnKf3hv.name: Expert Explorer (Ruins of Azlant)

hsa840pQNkcR9X5c.description:
Your first thought upon awaking in the asylum isn’t confusion over why you can’t remember anything clearly—it’s a sudden urge to find safety. You feel like everything around you, aside from your companions, might threaten you and you’re always ready to react. You have a hard time staying still, and you’re constantly surveying your surroundings. Maybe you are paranoid, or maybe it’s just an overactive imagination, but whatever it is, you know that keeping these feelings honed will help you stay alive.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on initiative checks and a +1 trait bonus on Reflex saves.
hsa840pQNkcR9X5c.name: Twitchy (Strange Aeons)

htVNPV1dGnQRf7cY.description:
You interposed yourself between an innocent and a dangerous evil monster or force, lost a great deal of blood as a result, and barely survived. Now, your blood seems to replenish with blessed speed, a sure sign of Vildeis’s favor.

Requirements: Vildeis

Benefits: You recover twice as many hit points and points of ability damage as normal when you rest, or three times as many when you take total bed rest.
htVNPV1dGnQRf7cY.name: Resilient Martyr (Vildeis)

contextNotes.text: Dealmaker traitFind items for sale more valuable than typical for the community size, DC10, +10 per community size difference

htyTEYqM7b2P0Sau.description:
You have the air of a person with money, allowing you to buy and sell valuables that otherwise are too rich for the local community.

Benefits: Once per week, when looking for an item with a price greater than the local community’s base value (see page 461 of the Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook), make a DC 10 Diplomacy check; add 10 to the DC for each community size category between the current community and the community size with a base value sufficient for that item. Success means (with GM approval) you’re able to sell or purchase that item as if the local community were of sufficient size. Each additional attempt (successful or otherwise) in that community increases the DC by an additional +10.

Example

If you’re trying to find a +1 cloak of resistance (1,000 gp), you’d normally need to be in a small town (base value 1,000 gp) or larger to be able to find that item with little trouble. If you’re currently in a hamlet, that’s two community sizes away from a small town, so the Diplomacy DC is 30. If you went to a village and asked there, the DC would only be 20 because it’s only one community size away from a small town.
htyTEYqM7b2P0Sau.name: Dealmaker

contextNotes.text: If you succeed at a saving throw against a fear effect that would result in a lesser effect on a successful save (such as causing you to become shaken instead of panicked), you are instead unaffected.

hu8vEOyvmXgxNMFb.description:
Your pride is so strong and your confidence so sure that you can shake off magical attempts to shatter your resolve.

Requirements: Keleshite Human Heritage

Benefits: If you succeed at a Will saving throw against a fear effect that would result in a lesser effect on a successful save (such as causing you to become shaken instead of panicked), you are instead unaffected.
hu8vEOyvmXgxNMFb.name: Pride of the Diamond Empire (Human; Keleshite)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to create forgeries.

huLAoBN2qXhwRXQZ.description:
You grew up within a bureaucratic system where law reigns supreme; however, that has never stopped you from creating loopholes in ironclad contracts.

Requirements: Chelaxian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Linguistics checks to create forgeries.
huLAoBN2qXhwRXQZ.name: Glib Barrister (Human; Chelaxian)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against sentient undead.

huW4cJwooGwiajs0.description:
Your close association with the intelligent undead of Geb has imparted some of their otherworldly essence to you, and this essence helps you in your dealings with the undead.

Requirements: Geb

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks against sentient undead.
huW4cJwooGwiajs0.name: Friend of the Dead (Geb)

contextNotes.text: Whenever a creature attempts to read your mind, it has to attempt a will save opposed by your Wisdom or Charisma check (your choice). If the reader fails this save, she is shaken for 1 round. If she fails by 10 or more, she is instead frightened for 1 round, then shaken for 1 round.

huYjtjFEB0AFRhDq.description: Benefits: When a nonevil humanoid attempts to read your mind via a magical effect, the reader must attempt a Will save opposed by your Wisdom or Charisma check (your choice). If the reader fails this save, she is shaken for 1 round. If she fails by 10 or more, she is instead frightened for 1 round, then shaken for 1 round.
huYjtjFEB0AFRhDq.name: Horrifying Mind

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to the DC of any charm or compulsion that does not provide ongoing control and results in peaceful acts, such as calm emotions, sleep, or a suggestion to lay down arms.

huwKMmhlunLD55zw.description:
Your tact soothes bristling tempers and cools heated blood.

Requirements: Grundinnar

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks.

In addition, you receive a +1 trait bonus to the DC of any charm or compulsion that does not provide ongoing control and results in peaceful acts, such as calm emotions, sleep, or a suggestion to lay down arms.
huwKMmhlunLD55zw.name: Honeyed Words (Grundinnar)

contextNotes.text: Increase the range increment of ranged weapons (but not thrown weapons) you wield by 5 feet.

hwHQDXLXjdtIGg7S.description:
You learned to make the most of ranged attacks as you close with targets.

Benefits: Increase the range increment of ranged weapons (but not thrown weapons) you wield by 5 feet.
hwHQDXLXjdtIGg7S.name: Roving Range

hwsfmrZyhROj6jqA.description:
You’ve come to appreciate the balanced approach taken by sovereign dragons.

Benefits: Once per day, before attempting a saving throw against a spell ability originating from another creature, you can invoke the determination of those dragons. You take 10 on the saving throw (treat the d20 die result as if it were a 10).
hwsfmrZyhROj6jqA.name: Balanced Determination

i0yHyT36tLoiRusF.description:
You are a creature of pleasure and comfort.

Effects: Whenever you spend a day without gaining reward or treasure (at least 10 or more gold pieces) or spending an hour on entertainment or pleasure, attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save at the end of that day. If you fail, you begin the next day fatigued. This fatigue lasts 4 hours, or until you receive a reward or suff icient entertainment or pleasure.
i0yHyT36tLoiRusF.name: Hedonistic

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus outside of combat.

i1Rn2f1hATPQ1a6G.description:
You have experienced firsthand the horrors of war, which have left you physically or mentally scarred. Now you have committed yourself to the teachings of the Blessed Attendant, seeking to heal those wounded in combat and to prevent battles from occurring whenever you can.

Requirements: Dalenydra

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks outside of combat.

In addition, when you heal creatures other than yourself using class abilities, feats, spells, and spell-completion or spell-trigger magic items, you restore 1 additional hit point.
i1Rn2f1hATPQ1a6G.name: Scarred by War (Dalenydra)

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] Penalty against vermin
-[[2]] Penalty against the nauseated condition of a swarm’s distraction ability

i2dg4ocIHYSJk0zM.description:
A harrowing experience with insects when you were young instilled in you a deep-seated fear of vermin of all description, especially when they swarm together.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on attacks against vermin, and you take a –2 penalty on saving throws against the nauseated condition of a swarm’s distraction ability.
i2dg4ocIHYSJk0zM.name: Entomophobe

i6wTexoXQKv7rxon.description:
Your religious studies hinged on teachings rarely recognized by your faith.

Benefits: You can select one of the apocryphal subdomains detailed below, provided the subdomain is associated with your patron deity. In some cases, you can select a subdomain associated with a deity despite that deity not offering the associated domain—these exceptions are noted with an asterisk (*), and you gain domain powers and domain spells from the associated domain as usual, even though your deity normally doesn’t grant access to that domain. When selecting a subdomain associated with two domains, a priest can choose only a subdomain that modifies a domain to which he has access.

i6wTexoXQKv7rxon.name: Acolyte of Apocrypha

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus versus poison.

i8UUlVZcL31cnxVY.description:
You’ve swum with the devilfish along the Mediogaltian coast.

Requirements: Ilizmagorti

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws versus poison.
i8UUlVZcL31cnxVY.name: Devilfish Diver (Ilizmagorti)

i9PO2At7ghuhnw4j.description:
You know how to take advantage of enemies who are unprepared for your assault.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative checks. In addition, once per day when you make an attack of opportunity, you gain a +2 trait bonus on the attack roll.
i9PO2At7ghuhnw4j.name: Tactician

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in jungle terrain.

iAiVbV2nrV3oh5bO.description:
You have learned how to navigate the darkest jungles of the Mwangi Expanse, and how to avoid the most dangerous hazards of the land.

Requirements: Mwangi Expanse

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks in jungle terrain.
iAiVbV2nrV3oh5bO.name: Jungle Walker (Mwangi Expanse)

iGV95DTSvO2oAGuk.description:
You have lived in many cities dotting the northern coastline of Garundi, and after you have departed from each, you have taken a small token to remember it by.

Requirements: Garundi Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Sleight of Hand checks.
iGV95DTSvO2oAGuk.name: Ever Home (Human; Garundi)

iKEcRBRRL3dSzDLx.description:
Having been raised among other aasimars, you have a stronger connection to your planar heritage than most of your kind, and have more thoroughly explored your magical talent.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: You may expend your once per day spell-like ability to spontaneously cast cure light wounds (CL equal to your character level), but doing so is draining and you take an amount of nonlethal damage equal to half the number of hit points you heal.
iKEcRBRRL3dSzDLx.name: Celestial Community (Aasimar)

iLTHscV6zrWaUs8I.description:
Your ancestor was a bralani, an azata known for its untamed speed in battle. When in combat, you feel a fierce surge of energy through your limbs.

Requirements: Musetouched Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, you may move an additional 5 feet as part of a move action.
iLTHscV6zrWaUs8I.name: Bralani's Step (Aasimar; Musetouched)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when using one of Aquan, Auran, Ignan, or Terran to communicate with a creature originating from the language’s corresponding Elemental Plane.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when using one of Aquan, Auran, Ignan, or Terran to communicate with a creature originating from the language’s corresponding Elemental Plane.

iOFTJ2a5QSKpjlfN.description:
You have traveled to the Elemental Planes under the protection of a powerful group of merchants or spellcasters.

Benefits: At character creation, you learn one of the following languages as a bonus language: Aquan, Auran, Ignan, or Terran.

Additionally, your options for bonus languages from having a high Intelligence score include the other three elemental languages, in addition to those language options allowed by your race and class.

You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Bluff checks when using one of these languages to communicate with a creature originating from the language’s corresponding Elemental Plane.
iOFTJ2a5QSKpjlfN.name: Elemental Negotiator

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs sphinxes.
+[[1]] Trait bonus vs sphinxes.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to decipher a puzzle or riddle.

iOJIMRxnFnn27r9k.description:
You’ve always been fascinated with the ancient race of sphinxes, and are inspired by them to love puzzles and riddles and enjoy solving difficult dilemmas. Like so many others, you’ve come to Wati to explore its ancient necropolis, but you’ve also heard that sphinxes occasionally visit a sphinx-shaped ruin called Ubet’s Folly in the city—perhaps you’ll have the chance to meet and talk with a sphinx yourself!

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks against sphinxes, and a +1 trait bonus on any skill check to decipher a puzzle or riddle. In addition, you may choose Sphinx as one of your bonus languages.
iOJIMRxnFnn27r9k.name: Sphinx Riddler (Mummy's Mask)

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] Penalty against the distraction ability of swarms
-[[2]] Penalty against mind-affecting effects

iQIzPkiRztnbECMu.description:
When you were young, a relative with whom you had frequently quarreled passed away where his or her soul could not rest. Now, the unquiet spirit appears around you at inconvenient times, distracting you with regret for being unable to help.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on saving throws against the distraction ability of swarms and mind-affecting effects and on concentration checks.
iQIzPkiRztnbECMu.name: Haunting Regret

iQWziyNts4M0a4jJ.description:
There is a dark hunger in you that rejoices when you or an ally slays a foe.

Requirements: Dark Archive

Benefits: Once per day when an enemy creature is killed, you may, as an immediate action, gain a number of temporary hit points equal to the slain foe’s Hit Dice. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute. This is a supernatural ability.
iQWziyNts4M0a4jJ.name: Soul Drinker (Dark Archive)

iSF7ffJGMi6AoV3T.description:
Like the blue dragons your tribe reveres, you have a touch of illusory magic.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You are able to cast ghost sound twice per day as a spell-like ability. The caster level for this effect is equal to your character level. This spell-like ability’s save DC is Intelligence-based.
iSF7ffJGMi6AoV3T.name: Draconic Echo (Kobold)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against attacks made by lawful outsiders

iTRuZaleSYmsNDiL.description:
Learning how devils think helps you resist their trickery.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to your AC against attacks made by lawful outsiders.
iTRuZaleSYmsNDiL.name: Devil Fighter

iTtxpwODj2Riy1kG.description:
You are quick with your tongue and have always possessed the talent to quickly admonish your enemies.

Requirements: Kitharodian Academy

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks and Bluff is a class skill for you.
iTtxpwODj2Riy1kG.name: Witty Repartee (Kitharodian Academy)

contextNotes.text: You gain cold resistance 2; this resistance does not stack with cold resistance gained from any other source.

iVxaOYWolzdanROC.description:
One of your parents came from the North, and the tales of the frozen lands at the top of the world that you grew up listening to excited your imagination. Alternatively, maybe one of your ancestors passed on the blood of some frost-rimed creature. You feel most alive during the chill of winter, and as a child, you spent hours playing in the snow. You rarely feel the cold, and you’ve always had a restless longing to travel north.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves, as well as cold resistance 2; this resistance does not stack with cold resistance gained from any other source.
iVxaOYWolzdanROC.name: Northern Ancestry (Reign of Winter)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to hide the fact that you are drinking a potion.

iZjoWPKpcYtNZ3zg.description:
Priests of Razmiran often make subtle use of potions to hide the fact that they do not receive spells from their deity, and they have become quite adept at imbibing potions surreptitiously.

Requirements: Razmiran

Benefits: You have mastered these techniques; whenever you consume a potion, you can attempt a Sleight of Hand check with a +2 trait bonus opposed by onlookers’ Perception checks to hide the fact that you are drinking a potion (as if you were using Sleight of Hand to hide an object). The observer’s success doesn’t prevent you from drinking the potion, just from doing so unnoticed. This does not hide any visible effects that the potion might have on you, only the physical act of drinking the potion.
iZjoWPKpcYtNZ3zg.name: Palm Potion (Razmiran)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against Large or larger creatures of the animal type.

iZuKkal275sIZ9nv.description:
You have spent long hours hunting giant creatures and have become skilled at bringing them down quickly.

Requirements: Realm of the Mammoth Lords

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against Large or larger creatures of the animal type.
iZuKkal275sIZ9nv.name: Big Game Hunter (Realm of the Mammoth Lords)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against mind-affecting effects.

iatLeMTe5r0iPmf4.description:
Since Talmandor's Bounty is an Andoren colony and is unlikely to see the threat of slavery, the presence of the Eagle Knights isn’t strongly needed, and the Bountiful Venture Company would prefer to not have that organization immediately associated with the colony (despite the colony’s name having been derived from the Eagle Knights’ patron, Talmandor). However, the need for safety can’t be overlooked. You were a soldier in the Andoren army or you were a civilian who showed great promise in military matters. The Eagle Knights recruited you and offered unofficial Eagle Knight status if you managed to make it into the ranks of the colonists. There, you would see to the safety of the colony and send back reports with each supply ship regarding the general state of the colony and any threats you notice. The Bountiful Venture Company admired your military prowess and selected you for the second wave of colonists to arrive at Talmandor’s Bounty.

Benefits: You begin play with a breastplate, a longsword (or some other martial or simple weapon), and an additional 100 gp of gear. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects.
iatLeMTe5r0iPmf4.name: Eagle Knight Recruit (Ruins of Azlant)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against favored enemies that share one of your subtypes.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls against favored enemies that share one of your subtypes during suprise round.

ibexuH5hsU5hxJ49.description:
You are adept at tricking favored enemies of your race or those related to your race.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Bluff checks against favored enemies that share one of your subtypes. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made against such favored enemies during the surprise round.
ibexuH5hsU5hxJ49.name: Cold and Calculating

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus to determine awareness for the purpose of a surprise round.

ibxleEA7DwIugmOY.description:
You crossed the law (or those in charge thought you did), and you are now a wanted criminal. As such, you have grown used to looking over your shoulder wherever you go.

Benefits: While in settlements, you gain a +3 trait bonus on Perception checks to determine awareness for the purpose of a surprise round.
ibxleEA7DwIugmOY.name: Fugitive

contextNotes.text: You roll twice and take the lower result on Sense Motive checks to get hunches. You cannot reroll this result, even if you have another ability that would normally allow you to do so.

icG3C7h1HmbevxE6.description:
You were reported to a dangerous authority such as the Gray Gardeners of Galt, the high inquisitors of Cheliax, or the Council of Mwanyisa of Mzali, and narrowly escaped death. You second-guess your instincts constantly, leaving you never sure whether or not to trust someone and endangered if you encounter that group’s agents again.

Effects: You can roll twice and take the lower result on Sense Motive checks to get hunches. You cannot reroll this result, even if you have another ability that would normally allow you to do so.
icG3C7h1HmbevxE6.name: Betrayed (SH)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information about any person with levels in monk.

ihyCI3SlWPQ0vrxz.description:
You have a passion for history and news concerning monastic disciplines.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with your choice of either Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (history), and the one you choose is always a class skill for you. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made to gather information about any person with levels in monk.
ihyCI3SlWPQ0vrxz.name: Style Sage

contextNotes.text: Any creature that performs a blood drain or swallow whole attack on you must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = [[10 + @attributes.hd.level + @abilities.con.mod]]) or become infected with filth fever. This is a poison effect.

ii0tsjCwQ2qqgqZn.description:
Your work with infections has infused your spirit with disease.

Requirements: Ghlaunder

Benefits: Any creature that performs a blood drain or swallow whole attack on you must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + your character level + your Constitution modifier) or become infected with filth fever. This is a poison effect.
ii0tsjCwQ2qqgqZn.name: Diseased Heart (Ghlaunder)

im0JBzMqeObourHw.description:
Although you live some distance from the city-state your community owes fealty to, you’ve been influenced by its sophisticated urban ways.

Requirements

Varisia

Benefits

Determine the city-state of which your home is a holding. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with one Profession skill and one of the following benefits.

Riddleport

Gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks.
im0JBzMqeObourHw.name: Regional Influence - Riddleport (Varisia)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against the supernatural effects of channeled energy or abilities powered by ki.

in4lFybqucijaztk.description:
Growing up among acolytes or monks, you were exposed to their supernatural power.

Requirements: Any City

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the supernatural effects of channeled energy or abilities powered by ki, and regain 1 additional hit point when healed by supernatural effects.
in4lFybqucijaztk.name: Cloistered Upbringing (Any City)

contextNotes.text:
–[[1]] Penalty on saving throws against fire effects.
–[[1]] Penalty on all saving throws whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire until you spend an entire round away from fire
These penalties are not cumulative
–[[1]] Penalty on all attack rolls whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire until you spend an entire round away from fire
–[[1]] Penalty on all skill checks whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire until you spend an entire round away from fire

inS09OERLEwKLrsH.description:
You were badly burned once by volcanic ash, torch-wielding mobs, or some fiery accident, and the scars pain you terribly you whenever you are too near to fire.

Effects: You take a –1 penalty on saving throws against fire effects. In addition, whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire, you take a –1 penalty on all attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks until you spend an entire round away from fire. These penalties are not cumulative. (An instantaneous fire effect adjacent to you or affecting you causes this penalty to apply until 1 round after it is gone.)
inS09OERLEwKLrsH.name: Burned

io9TgjprRYuLUhie.description:
Your exploration of the magic for calling genies has increased your overall aptitude for summoning.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: Once per day, you may cast one conjuration (summoning) spell as if your caster level were 2 higher than normal.
io9TgjprRYuLUhie.name: Genie-Caller (Qadira)

contextNotes.text: You can disarm magic traps.

it4fqP0SZX1oE5PS.description:
Forgotten dungeons and ancient tombs have always held an appeal for you, and you’ve never been able to resist the urge to delve into these lost sites in search of knowledge, treasure, or both. You may not have received any formal training in the roguish arts, but you’ve nonetheless become skilled at spotting and disabling hidden traps. The tombs of Wati’s necropolis, just opened for exploration, seem like the perfect place to put your skills to the test.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disable Device checks, and that skill is always a class skill for you. In addition, you can use Disable Device to disarm magic traps, like a rogue.
it4fqP0SZX1oE5PS.name: Trap Finder (Mummy's Mask)

iuAj0u3TZ6uiZ1Fg.description:
The spirit voices of your ancestors sing to you of the world around you.

Requirements: Bekyar Human Heritage or Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, you can act in the surprise round even if you aren’t aware of your attackers; you can use this action only to start a bardic song or raging song, and you’re still considered flat-footed until you act in the first normal round of combat.
iuAj0u3TZ6uiZ1Fg.name: Shoanti Spirit-singer (Human; Bekyar or Shoanti)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs enchantment spells and spell-like abilities.

j0jl7b3NJ7BmQeoJ.description:
You resist attempts to dissuade you from obeying the Decemvirate’s will.

Requirements: Grand Lodge

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against enchantment spells and spelllike abilities.
j0jl7b3NJ7BmQeoJ.name: Loyalty (Grand Lodge)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks relating to Sargavan settlements and politics.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against disease.

j1TB4nwlioMXWveR.description:
You come from a long line of Sargavan colonists, stretching back to Cheliax's original expansion into the land during the Everwar. While your people's control of Sargava wanes every day, and Eleder has functioned independently from Cheliax since House Thrune brought diabolism to the political forefront, you nevertheless maintain a hardy Chelish constitution and natural sense of survival. What took you away from home is up to you, but you return to the land of your birth on the Jenivere in the hope of putting Sargava back on the map.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks relating to Sargavan settlements and politics and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against disease. Characters with this trait are Sargavan natives and might be returning via any city, but especially from the Chelish ports of Kintargo, Pezzack, or Corentyn.
j1TB4nwlioMXWveR.name: Colonial (Serpent's Skull)

j4zXyyxLBieQTBNb.description:
You have learned the ways of many lands.

Exemplar Type: Regional Exemplar

Benefits: Choose any two skills; each of those skills becomes a class skill for you. For every other regional trait you have, select an additional skill to treat as a class skill.

Exemplar Traits: A character can have only one exemplar trait, and selecting an exemplar trait takes the place of two regular traits. Each exemplar trait is tied to a trait category, such as combat traits or regional traits; a character with an exemplar trait is no longer restricted to a single trait of that category and can select any number of such traits when gaining further traits. In addition, a character with an exemplar trait can select the Additional Traits feat any number of times and use the feat to gain an exemplar trait of a valid trait category for which they qualify.
j4zXyyxLBieQTBNb.name: Traveler of a Hundred Lands

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty vs illusions.

j85VYhjNWD2Eeq8D.description:
You’ve told too many lies, and made up too many cover stories, and now even you can’t keep it all straight. The lies have become reality to you, which makes it difficult to think of a new convincing lie. You hesitate at crucial moments when trying to fool those around you, and likely shouldn’t be in the field at all anymore.

Effects: You take a -2 penalty on Bluff checks and saving throws against illusions.
j85VYhjNWD2Eeq8D.name: Too Many Secrets

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to use the aid another action.

jBhI5gBUfsoguDv5.description:
You were forced to get by in an isolated enclave, whether surrounded by a community that feared and suspected you or in a remote locale far from other people, and you became practiced at assisting your friends.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on skill checks to use the aid another action.

Suggested Characters

Halflings, Mwangi, Tians
jBhI5gBUfsoguDv5.name: Supportive

contextNotes.text: When making a long jump, you treat the DC of the check as if the distance being jumped were 5 feet shorter than it actually is.

jEGy03vJZ71x7fE1.description:
You’ve spent more than your fair share of time among the strange sloping slums that cover the rooftops of much of Old Korvosa.

Requirements: Korvosa in Varisia

Benefits: When using the Acrobatics skill to make a long jump, you treat the DC of the skill check as if the distance being jumped were 5 feet shorter than it actually is.
jEGy03vJZ71x7fE1.name: Roof Racer (Varisia; Korvosa)

contextNotes.text: When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

jHjiByVBBGf4szEU.description:
Your family is extremely important to you, and you feel disheartened when you can't do what they ask.

Effects: When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a –2 penalty on all Wisdom- and Charisma-based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day. You can't take this drawback if you have no family. If you ever lose your family or lose contact with your family, exchange this drawback for the Doubt drawback.
jHjiByVBBGf4szEU.name: Family Ties

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls, and a +[[2]] Trait bonus to confirm critical hits, against creatures with the giant subtype.

jIuHWW9Q92LOZuSb.description:
You grew up in a tranquil, happy home near the mountains, but that peace was shattered when giants came down from the mountains to raid your settlement. The giants killed your parents and left you a young orphan, and since that day, you’ve sworn to avenge the deaths of your kin.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against creatures with the giant subtype, and a +2 trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits against creatures with the giant subtype.
jIuHWW9Q92LOZuSb.name: Orphaned by Giants (Giantslayer)

jKaDBKnYkbs6VNjz.description:
Your total indifference to the law even as you’re breaking it is such that those watching you are stunned.

Benefits: Whenever you commit an unexpected and outrageously unlawful act (such as knowingly destroying or stealing something) that would elicit a hostile response from another creature, you may attempt a Bluff check opposed by the opponent’s Sense Motive check. If you’re successful, you and any allies who were willfully expecting your unlawful deed may act in a surprise round.

For each ally willfully expecting your unlawful deed, you take a cumulative –2 penalty on your Bluff check. If your Bluff check fails, there is no surprise round. Regardless of whether your Bluff check succeeds, you and any allies willfully taking advantage of your unlawful deed treat your initiative checks for the duration of combat as though you had each rolled a 1.
jKaDBKnYkbs6VNjz.name: Unabashed Gall

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against outsiders belonging to the evil subtype.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against outsiders belonging to the evil subtype.

jKciMLe7NLqt7Qaz.description:
Your dark ancestry gives you a special insight into how to motivate or placate fiends and their kin.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks made against outsiders belonging to the evil subtype.
jKciMLe7NLqt7Qaz.name: Family Connections (Tiefling)

jOfj8WFHTIAj0Y5l.description:
You are skilled at fighting with objects not traditionally considered weapons.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls with improvised weapons.
jOfj8WFHTIAj0Y5l.name: Surprise Weapon

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells and spell-like abilities cast by humans.

jPzZnv6fYH4zXo5x.description:
Born during a lunar eclipse, you were forever marked by the moon’s mysterious and ancient magic.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the spells and spell-like abilities cast by humans.
jPzZnv6fYH4zXo5x.name: Lunar Birth

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus while underwater.
Once per day, you can attempt to hide in plain sight while underwater. This is only effective against creatures that rely on blindsense, blindsight, or tremorsense.

jQXbeY1eQ9HURqsQ.description:
For whatever reason, you spent much of your formative years fighting to keep yourself alive amid the fearsome creatures that inhabit the infinite oceans of the Plane of Water, and you learned how to stay hidden even against predators with unusual senses.

Requirements: Plane of Water

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Stealth checks while underwater. Once per day, you can attempt a Stealth check to hide in plain sight (as the ranger class feature of the same name) while underwater. This is only effective against creatures that rely on blindsense, blindsight, or tremorsense. Creatures with other senses perceive you normally.
jQXbeY1eQ9HURqsQ.name: Aquatic Survivalist (Plane of Water)

jWD1MoLUyUpDAEAC.description:
You’ve studied the speeches and writings of a leader or thinker who served as a reassuring voice in her community in the turbulent times after Aroden died, painfully learning which portions of Aroden’s advice no longer applied and what of his writings could still be usefully applied.

Benefits: Once per day, you can speak Aroden’s words of encouragement to grant any human a +1 insight bonus on any attack roll, saving throw, or skill check of his choice within the following hour. The choice to use the insight bonus must be made before the related roll is made.
jWD1MoLUyUpDAEAC.name: Reassuring Advice

jZma1Ler6jlkkXpK.description:
From a very young age, the ability to master performance types has come quite naturally to you.

Requirements: Kitharodian Academy

Benefits: Chose a performance type. You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Perform checks made using that performance type. Perform is always a class skill for you.
jZma1Ler6jlkkXpK.name: Savant (Kitharodian Academy)

contextNotes.text: You can make Bluff checks to create a diversion for allies within 30 feet to hide (provided they readied an action to hide), and you can make Bluff checks to feint for one adjacent ally of your choice. Either use of this ability is a standard action.

jaSNcX0ISJxoiWyT.description:
You can get anyone’s attention, even when they are focused elsewhere.

Requirements: Gathlain

Benefits: You can make Bluff checks to create a diversion for allies within 30 feet to hide (provided they readied an action to hide), and you can make Bluff checks to feint for one adjacent ally of your choice. Either use of this ability is a standard action.
jaSNcX0ISJxoiWyT.name: Frustrating (Gathlain)

jfV9NF44j86onVIn.description:
Your family comes from a long, proud tradition of housebreaking and thievery. You are a strong part of that tradition.

Requirements: The Exchange
